title: Chickpea Curry
description: This authentic Indian chickpea curry is a hearty, one-pot meal bursting with aromatic spices and rich coconut milk. Featuring tender chickpeas, caramelized onions, and fresh herbs, this naturally vegan and gluten-free dish delivers restaurant-quality flavors that will warm your soul and satisfy your cravings.
ingredients: Pinch baking soda (to caramelize onions quickly)
2 (14 oz) cans diced fire-roasted tomatoes (or regular diced tomatoes)
1/2 teaspoon sea salt, or more to taste
2 (16 oz) cans chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Fresh lime juice (of one lime, about 2 tablespoons)
Hot cooked basmati rice, for serving
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons coconut oil in a large, deep skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add 1 diced large onion and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until softened. Reduce heat to medium, add a pinch of baking soda, and stir well. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the onion is deep golden brown and caramelized, about 10 minutes. Don't rush this step—properly caramelized onions are the flavor foundation of the dish.

Stir in 4-5 minced garlic cloves and 2 tablespoons fresh minced ginger and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add 1 teaspoon mustard seeds, 1 teaspoon cumin seeds, and 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes. Cook for 1-2 minutes until the mustard seeds begin to pop. Stand back slightly when seeds start popping. Stir in 2 tablespoons tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently, until it darkens slightly.

Add 2 cans diced tomatoes with their juices, 2 teaspoons ground coriander, 2 teaspoons garam masala, 1 teaspoon turmeric, and 1/2 teaspoon salt, stirring to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan. Add 2 cans drained and rinsed chickpeas and stir to combine. Bring to a simmer.

In a small bowl, whisk 1 teaspoon cornstarch into 1 can coconut milk until smooth to prevent lumps. Shake the can well before opening as cream and liquid often separate. Pour into the pot and stir well. Simmer uncovered over medium-low heat for 25-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened to your liking.
Remove from heat. Stir in 1 cup of the chopped cilantro and squeeze 2 tablespoons fresh lime juice over the curry. Taste and adjust salt and spices as needed.
Ladle over hot cooked basmati rice. Garnish with the remaining 1/2 cup fresh cilantro and serve with naan and a small scoop of raita or tzatziki on the side, if desired.
notes: For gluten-free: This recipe is naturally gluten-free when served with rice. Ensure naan is gluten-free or substitute with gluten-free flatbread.
